key_insight: WeldMamba predicts future weld-pool regions from past images, process parameters, and sensor signals to compensate for control delay in laser wire-feed welding.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable real-time closed-loop welding control by providing lookahead segmentation of keyhole, wire, and molten-pool states.
method: Physics-guided spatiotemporal state space model for future semantic segmentation
Fuses coaxial grayscale video with welding parameters and aligned wire-state electrical signals
Uses temporal history, horizon-conditioned prediction, and motion-aware decoding with geometry-aware auxiliary losses
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate time alignment and sensor availability, so robustness may drop if signals are noisy or deployment timing shifts.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
